trivia_bullets.text: Wombat is one of Magic's rarest creature types. Introduced on Rabid Wombat in the 1994 set Legends, the type vanished from black-border cards until Cursed Wombat in MH3 — 30 years later. A silver-border detour came via Surgeon General Commander (Unsanctioned, 2020). MTG R&D designer Mark Gottlieb is noted for championing more wombat representation in Magic. [Wombat – MTG Wiki via WebSearch]
sources: Wombat – MTG Wiki via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Cursed Wombat is a Nightmare Wombat (2/3), making it simultaneously the type's first black-border return and its first Nightmare subtype. Its counter-proliferation ability functions similarly to Corpsejack Menace and Branching Evolution but deploys earlier at {2}{B}{G}. [Dexerto: 'MTG Modern Horizons 3's Cursed Wombat is a great Golgari counter booster']
sources: Dexerto: 'MTG Modern Horizons 3's Cursed Wombat is a great Golgari counter booster'

trivia_bullets.text: Cursed Wombat has become a Pauper EDH commander staple heading counter-based {B}{G} decks. Community framing at Tuesday Night Takeover describes it as 'basically a different version of Pauper EDH legend Corpsejack Menace.' EDHREC tracks approximately 8,696 Commander decks including the card. [tuesdaynighttakeover.com: 'Cursed Wombat Counters Pauper EDH'; EDHREC: cursed-wombat]

Controlling multiple Cursed Wombats stacks the counter-trigger. Per Scryfall rulings: adapting one wombat with two copies in play results in non-redundant trigger stacking across every instance. [Scryfall rulings via WebSearch snippet]

related_cards.name: Rabid Wombat
related_cards.note: 1994 origin of the Wombat type; 30-year predecessor

related_cards.name: Corpsejack Menace
related_cards.note: functional predecessor for the extra-counter effect; direct Pauper EDH comparison

related_cards.name: Branching Evolution
related_cards.note: stronger but more expensive doubling effect in the same strategic space
